RT @easya_app: our founders @kwok_phil and @dom_kwok sit down with the @solana team at 12pm ET today to chat all about our @consensus2026 h…

🚨Everything you need to know about @DriftProtocol incident recovery

- Drift Protocol is targeting full user recovery, not partial payouts
- Recovery backed by up to $150M from @Tether, ~$147M secured
- Funds are being placed into a dedicated recovery pool
- A structured payout model is being finalized based on user losses
- Recovery expected in phases, not a single distribution
- Protocol revenue will be used to support repayments over time
- Stolen funds are being tracked and pursued for additional recovery
- Coordinating with @SolanaFndn 
- Platform will relaunch only after a full security overhaul
- New risk controls and safeguards will be implemented to prevent future incidents

BREAKING : @driftprotocol gets $148 million funding from Tether and partners as it replaces Circle USDC with USDT after massive exploit 🚨

 good chances for compensation for affected users from the exploit ✨

WE ARE BACK!

Drift have now detailed a recovery initiative in collaboration with partners including @tether, totalling just under $150M.

A meaningful portion of exchange revenue, alongside committed support capital, will be allocated toward a dedicated user recovery pool.

We're working closely with the Drift team to understand the full details of how this will be rolled out, and will share further updates as soon as we have clarity.

More to follow.

We told our community we would find a path to recovery. This is that path. This is the first step toward making users whole over time and toward building back stronger than where we were before.
We are publishing an Incident Recovery Update that outlines all of these specific steps towards relaunch and user recovery in more detail. You can read it here: https://t.co/zOod9tknEI

Today, Drift is announcing a collaboration with @tether and other partners totaling up to nearly $150 million to support our commitment to a relaunch with USDT at the center, and a path to user recovery.

These funds encompass a $100M revenue-linked credit facility, an ecosystem grant, and loans to market makers, designed to fund a dedicated user recovery pool.

Learn more 👇

Relentless execution. Best-in-class Perps DEX experience.

Fire with Bullet.

Like and RT the tweet below if you found the thread helpful 👇

Here is the full Bullet List of upgrades and more:

Infra and Backend:
• Upgrade bullet code for Celestia's V8 upgrade
• Upgrade frontend to use Pyth Lazer
• Migrate to PagerDuty for oncall alerting
• Isolated margin functionality merged to backend code, allowing users to assign a specific amount of capital for individual trade

Product and Frontend:
• Oracle chart upgraded to real-time streaming. The oracle price chart now streams live price updates continuously, so candles refresh automatically without any page interaction.
• 24-hour price changes powered by CoinGecko. Market price change data is now sourced from CoinGecko, updating every 60 seconds
• Automatic deposit recovery. If a bridge deposit was interrupted or left incomplete, the app will now detect it on startup and automatically finalize it for you.
• Animated Bullet loading spinner. The chart loading screen now displays the Bullet logo animation instead of the default spinner.
• Price chart gaps now shown accurately. When there is no trading activity during a candle interval, the chart now correctly displays an empty gap instead of connecting unrelated candles.
• Fix mid price in the order form. The mid price (used when clicking "Mid" in the order form) is now correctly rounded to the market's tick size, so the value you see is always a valid price for that market.
- Take Profit / Stop Loss edits now work correctly. Fixed an issue where editing a TP/SL order could fail when only one side had a trigger price set.

It has been a fruitful few weeks with the Genesis Cup event.

We have also been cooking up many other developments during this period.

Let's dive into it 👇 https://t.co/Dhx5DWCxL0

The Solana stablecoin landscape has grown tremendously over the years

2024: $1.883B
2025: $5.475B
2026: $14.607B

That’s +190% YoY from 2024 → 2025, and another ~167% YoY into 2026.

We now have way more capital sitting onchain, a wave of new stablecoins entering the ecosystem, and a lot more ways to actually use them.

Solana is becoming the hub for stablecoins

12 more protocols got hacked since the $280M Drift exploit

Here’s what the last 2 weeks looked like:

> CoW Swap: frontend/DNS hijack

> Hyperbridge: forged message exploit
1B tokens minted, price → zero

> Bybit: $1B exploit attempt
Blocked in time

> KuCoin: $9.5M laundered

> Kraken: insider extortion attempt
2,000+ accounts exposed

> Dango: smart contract bug
$410K drained

> Silo V2: oracle exploit
$392K lost

> BSC TMM: reserve manipulation
$1.67M lost

> Aethir / SubQuer: access control
$480K combined

> MONA: burn address exploit
$61K lost

> Zerion: social engineering
$100K drained

Overall in 2026 around 45 protocols hacked
$450M+ lost in total

What’s actually the weakest link right now - code, people, or the system itself?

Proof of Solana OG. Receipts from 2021:

Back in 2021, I was sharing Excel sheets on Twitter with floor prices from nft collections.

I was living in Bali and had become an nft whale across all the first Solana OG collections. I probably only had 1k-4k followers at the time, and I was spending hours every single day building those sheets just to gain a slight edge in trading.

Everything was manua lmao. I had to collect data from different Solana marketplaces one by one.

There were no charts back then. No dashboards. No fancy analytics.

So every day, at the exact same time, I would log the numbers myself just to create consistent 24h data points.

The funny part is that only like 5-20 people were actively engaging with those posts.

I was pouring insane amounts of energy into my content with zero guarantee that anyone would see it, appreciate it, or care.

Now, looking back at my journey with 240k more followers, I finally understand why I had to go through all of that.

What a blessing, man. I was literally just a kid in this video.

I hope you guys understand that the efforts you make today can be incredibly meaningful, even if you can’t see the results yet.

Blockworks Advisory just released their Solana Q1 2026 Report.

Here’s everything you need to know 👇

➤ Network
- 10.1B non-vote transactions: NEW ATH, up from 8.9B in Q2 2025
- 1,303 TPS peak throughput, NEW ATH
- During the Feb 5 $2B liquidation event, ETH fees spiked to $8.67 (+500x). Solana barely moved.

➤ Markets
- $284.5B in DEX spot volume with 41% market share, ahead of Ethereum and all L2s combined
- Prop AMMs now control 62% of spot DEX volume
- Stablecoin transfer volume hit $2.1T (+60% QoQ and YoY)
- Tokenized asset volumes hit $1.3B ATH (+164% QoQ) led by xStocks and PreStocks

➤ Institutional
- SOL ETPs recorded $208M in net inflows vs. $198M in ETH ETP outflows
- On a market cap-adjusted basis, SOL attracted ~4x more inflows per dollar of market cap than BTC

➤ RWA & Lending
- Solana surpassed Ethereum in RWA lending deposits: $1.23B vs. ETH’s $1.13B (+115% QoQ)
- Total lending deposits across Kamino + Jup Lend: $4.39B

➤ App Revenue
$292M in app revenue in Q1 led by:
- Pumpfun ($123M)
- Axiom ($58M)
- Phantom ($33M)
- Jupiter ($14M)

BREAKING: We're partnering with @SolanaFndn to rebuild Solana's read layer from the ground up.

@anza_xyz and @jump_firedancer have done incredible work scaling execution and networking, but the read layer has stayed largely unchanged since genesis. It was built alongside the validator and never got its own architecture.

By 2026, that gap shows: slower access, expensive customisation, and growing limitations at scale. The teams closest to the problem built great tools behind closed doors because the read path was too deeply coupled to the validator to improve without massive effort.

It's time Solana's data access layer matched the ecosystem's needs, and we're proud to be the ones building it:

Big news: reads are moving out of Agave into two modular systems, independently scalable, in sync with the network tip, open-source and managed by @SolanaFndn:

- Accounts: an adaptive indexing engine that ingests, stores, and serves the exact account data your app needs at extremely low latency

- Ledger: full architecture to ingest, store, and serve the entire ledger faster and more efficiently in a columnar engine purpose-designed for how builders query data

Every infrastructure provider, builder, dApp, and institution benefits, with the biggest impact coming from what gets built on top.

Full architecture overview: https://t.co/yLMDZIoP9Z

More technical posts coming as we build through 2026, so make sure to follow us on X and subscribe to our blog.

- Higher subscription limits per connection
